title: Scenario Foundation
description: v1 ADDITIVE draft output contract for the deterministic, coverage-aware scenario FOUNDATION (task M5-T001). A scenario is assembled by deterministic code from a property_profile (1.4.0) and a rule_evaluation (1.0.0) result consumed READ-ONLY; it performs NO independent legal calculation. When (and only when) the R5 residential-FAR family is applicable and conditional with no conflict/uncertainty, the scenario SURFACES the canonical rule_evaluation trace output max_residential_floor_area_sq_ft VERBATIM as draft_zoning_floor_area_cap_sq_ft - a DRAFT maximum residential ZONING-FLOOR-AREA CAP under ZR 23-21, NEVER recomputed here and NEVER relabeled as gross/net/sellable/feasible area or a buildable envelope. Every candidate constraint carries exactly one ConstraintCompleteness state (known/draft/missing/conflicting/unsupported/professional_review_required) plus a PRD-section-12 data-completeness status and its provenance; envelope constraints (height, stories, setbacks, yards, lot coverage, open space, parking, street wall, use group, overlays, density bonuses) are MISSING and MUST NOT be inferred. A scenario_kind of no_scenario is produced (fail-closed) on any conflict, professional-review/spatial uncertainty, malformed/non-finite input, or absent controlling input; unsupported districts yield a visible unsupported stub. coverage_status is the canonical PRD-section-12 vocabulary REFERENCED from coverage_status.schema.json and NARROWED to exclude 'verified' (allOf + subset enum) - a scenario is NEVER Verified; every scenario carries not_verified_disclaimer and needs_review. Any far*lot_area recompute is VERIFICATION-ONLY (integrity_check), never the surfaced value, and fails closed (data_conflict, no cap) on disagreement. Preliminary scenarios vary ONLY via explicit typed assumptions (no hidden utilization/optimization defaults). The evaluated input is identified BY REFERENCE (bbl + profile/rule_evaluation contract versions + input_fingerprint); this document NEVER embeds a full property profile.

contract_version.description: CLOSED enum of published scenario contract versions. 1.0.0 = M5-T001 initial foundation shape. New versions are admitted only by an accepted contract task that appends to this enum.

scenario_kind.description: The typed outcome. 'preliminary' = a draft zoning-floor-area cap was surfaced from the canonical R5 trace (never a buildable envelope). 'no_scenario' = fail-closed (conflict, professional review, spatial uncertainty, malformed/non-finite input, or an absent controlling input); no cap is surfaced. 'unsupported' = the district/rule family is not implemented; a visible stub with reasons and no cap.

data_completeness.description: The most-severe PRD-section-12 data-completeness across the scenario's constraints (referenced from coverage_status.schema.json, never redefined). A preliminary scenario is honestly missing_critical: the envelope-governing families (height, setbacks/yards, lot coverage/open space) have no rule and are MISSING, so even with the draft cap present the data is critically incomplete for a buildable envelope.

needs_review.description: Always true: every scenario inherits the draft (needs_review) M4 lineage and is never auto-final. A scenario is never Verified.

professional_review_required.description: True when the outcome escalates to a qualified human (professional-review coverage, spatial uncertainty, or a fail-closed malformed-input stop). Deterministic escalation, never an AI decision.

not_verified_disclaimer.description: The permanent honest disclaimer stamped on every scenario. Its human-readable text may contain the word 'Verified'; that text is NEVER a coverage_status and never up-labels the scenario.

constraints.description: Every candidate constraint the scenario could use, each with exactly one ConstraintCompleteness state - nothing silently absent. Emitted in a fixed deterministic order.

draft_zoning_floor_area_cap_sq_ft.description: The canonical rule_evaluation trace output max_residential_floor_area_sq_ft surfaced VERBATIM (never recomputed here) on a preliminary scenario; null on every no_scenario/unsupported outcome and whenever the integrity check fails closed. Strictly positive when present.

cap_label.description: The mandatory label attached to a surfaced cap (present only on a preliminary scenario, null otherwise): it states the value is a DRAFT residential zoning-floor-area cap under ZR 23-21 and explicitly NOT gross/net/sellable/feasible area or a buildable envelope.

cap_provenance.description: Provenance of the surfaced cap propagated verbatim from the consumed rule_evaluation trace (rule_id/version/status + the rule citation(s) with their source-snapshot provenance); null when no cap is surfaced.

assumptions.description: Explicitly declared typed assumptions applied to this scenario. DEFAULTS TO EMPTY. A preliminary scenario with no assumptions equals the raw draft cap; there are NO implicit utilization, efficiency, yield, or optimization factors anywhere in the path. Any variation exists ONLY as an explicit entry here and NEVER changes draft_zoning_floor_area_cap_sq_ft (the surfaced cap is always the raw canonical value).

reasons.description: Human-readable reasons for the outcome (fail-safe cause, unsupported explanation, named missing constraint, integrity-disagreement, etc.). Never a substitute for the machine-readable fields above.

coverage_matrix.description: The rule-coverage dependency matrix (proposal section 7): what must exist before a scenario may be presented as a real buildable envelope. Emitted on every scenario in a fixed order; everything but the R5 residential-FAR row is MISSING and must not be inferred.

_expected_failure.description: FIXTURE-ONLY annotation stating the intended defect of an invalid fixture (packages/contracts README convention). The builder NEVER emits this key; it is allowed here (optional) so an invalid fixture fails only for its stated defect under additionalProperties:false, and valid fixtures - which omit it - stay clean.

coverage_status_draft.description: The canonical PRD-section-12 coverage vocabulary (REFERENCED from coverage_status.schema.json, never redefined) NARROWED to exclude 'verified'. A scenario may never be Verified (PRD sections 10-12). The allOf keeps coverage_status.schema.json the single source of the vocabulary while the subset enum removes exactly 'verified'.

evaluated_input.description: Identity of the evaluated input BY REFERENCE - never an embedded copy of the property profile. bbl + the evaluated profile contract_version + the consumed rule_evaluation contract_version + the deterministic input_fingerprint (carried verbatim from the rule_evaluation) pin exactly which inputs produced this scenario without storing the profile.

bbl.description: BBL of the evaluated property, or null when the inputs carried no identity.bbl.

profile_contract_version.description: contract_version of the property_profile instance that was consumed (e.g. '1.4.0'). Open non-empty string, not coupled to the profile contract's evolving enum.

rule_evaluation_contract_version.description: contract_version of the rule_evaluation document that was consumed (e.g. '1.0.0').

input_fingerprint.description: The rule_evaluation.evaluated_input.input_fingerprint carried verbatim (a sha256: hex digest), or null when the consumed document carried none. Lets a consumer confirm the scenario came from a specific input snapshot WITHOUT storing the profile.

constraint.description: One candidate constraint with exactly one ConstraintCompleteness state, its provenance, and its PRD-section-12 data-completeness. A 'missing' constraint records a gap and may NEVER be inferred or defaulted.

key.description: Stable machine key for the constraint family (e.g. residential_far_cap, lot_area, height_limit).

state.description: ConstraintCompleteness (proposal section 4). 'known' = present from an authoritative source; 'draft' = from a needs_review rule (never Verified); 'missing' = no rule/datum provides it (may not be inferred); 'conflicting' = sources/rules disagree; 'unsupported' = family not implemented; 'professional_review_required' = spatial uncertainty / fail-safe.

value.description: The constraint value when present, else null. Never NaN/Infinity (strict JSON).

unit.description: Unit of value when numeric (e.g. square_feet), else null.

provenance.description: Provenance propagated verbatim from the source contract (rule citations + rule_id/version/status for the cap; profile field + dataset for lot area), or null when the constraint is a recorded gap with no source.

note.description: Human-readable note (e.g. why the constraint is missing and that it must not be inferred).

cap_provenance.description: Provenance of the surfaced draft cap, propagated verbatim from the consumed rule_evaluation trace. Never invented here.

output_name.description: The canonical trace output key the cap was taken from (max_residential_floor_area_sq_ft).

citation.description: One source citation WITH its resolved source-snapshot provenance, carried verbatim from the rule_evaluation trace (a material value may never be surfaced without it, PRD section 19).

assumption.description: One explicitly declared typed assumption. It is recorded verbatim and NEVER silently applied to the surfaced cap; a variant scenario differs from the raw draft-cap scenario only by the presence of entries here.

assumption_type.description: Open label for the kind of assumption (e.g. utilization_factor, efficiency_ratio). Recorded, never defaulted.

coverage_matrix_row.description: One row of the rule-coverage dependency matrix (proposal section 7).

rule_status_today.description: draft = a needs_review rule exists (only the R5 residential-FAR cap today); missing = no rule exists and the family MUST NOT be inferred; out_of_scope = not a zoning rule / outside R5 scope.

integrity_check.description: The VERIFICATION-ONLY integrity check (proposal section 5 step 5). When a preliminary cap is a candidate, the engine MAY recompute far*lot_area purely to COMPARE against the canonical trace value. It NEVER replaces the surfaced value and MUST FAIL CLOSED (data_conflict no_scenario, no cap) on disagreement beyond the documented tolerance. Raw numbers are NOT surfaced here on disagreement.

performed.description: True when the recompute-and-compare ran (i.e. both far and a positive lot area were available).

agreed.description: True when the verification recompute matched the canonical trace value within tolerance; false when it disagreed (fail-closed to no_scenario); null when the check was not performed.

tolerance.description: The documented absolute-or-relative tolerance used for the comparison.

method.description: Fixed description of the comparison method (e.g. 'abs(recomputed - canonical) <= tolerance * max(1, abs(canonical))').
